jokes.text: Oh, that south. You never know what he'll do next.
jokes.setup: Homer reading the bridge column in newspaper
jokes.punchline: Treating bridge direction 'South' as an unpredictable character
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding - he doesn't understand bridge terminology and anthropomorphizes compass directions. Good character integration.

jokes.text: He's like an unfunny version of me.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on comic strip character
jokes.punchline: Homer calling someone else unfunny while being oblivious to his own lack of humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-awareness from Homer who lacks any self-awareness. The humor comes from character blindness.

jokes.text: Probably digging for drugs.
jokes.setup: Bart is outside digging
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate assumption is drugs
jokes.explanation: Standard dark humor about parental paranoia, but fits Homer's character reasonably well.

jokes.text: What are you doing? / Digging. Why? / To make a hole. / A hole for what? / More digging.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to understand Bart's activity
jokes.punchline: Circular logic - digging to dig more
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist exchange with perfect escalating circular logic. Economy is superb - maximum funny per word.

jokes.text: So, I guess you wouldn't mind if I was to dig a hole of my own? / Go for it. Maybe I Will. / What's stopping you? Very little.
jokes.setup: Homer trying reverse psychology
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission that 'very little' is stopping him
jokes.explanation: Homer's failed attempt at psychology backfires perfectly when he admits his own limitations honestly.

jokes.text: I'm having chest pains. / Where's the defibrillator? Clear! / This thing pays for itself.
jokes.setup: Homer having chest pains from minor exertion
jokes.punchline: Casual use of defibrillator and cost-benefit analysis
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical emergency treated casually, with good economic punchline about the defibrillator.

jokes.text: My dad's always yelling that whitey's keeping him down.
jokes.setup: Child psychologist asking about angry people in house
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual reference to Homer's paranoid rants
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how normalized Homer's bizarre behavior is to his family.

jokes.text: If it happens, it happens.
jokes.setup: Being told he'll dig through to China
jokes.punchline: Bart's complete nonchalance about impossible scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd acceptance. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Those inscrutable Americans. What are they up to now?
jokes.setup: Cut to China showing hole appearing
jokes.punchline: Chinese person calling Americans inscrutable (reverse of stereotype)
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of 'inscrutable Oriental' stereotype. Good visual payoff to digging setup.

jokes.text: We all know you're the great humungus! / Well, I'm just saying... / Oh, you're always 'just saying.'
jokes.setup: Homer telling bar story about being 'great humungus'
jokes.punchline: Patrons are tired of his repetitive bragging
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer as annoying bar regular with repetitive stories.

jokes.text: Well, I just naturally assumed.
jokes.setup: Asked how he knew about Chinese spying
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission he made it all up
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical casual dishonesty delivered with perfect nonchalance.

jokes.text: And I've read the entire sweet valley high series.
jokes.setup: Moe calling Homer's story the stupidest he's heard
jokes.punchline: Moe has read teen girl romance novels
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation - tough bartender Moe reading teen romance novels is perfectly unexpected yet believable.

jokes.text: Sorry, shaggy.
jokes.setup: Moe complaining about 'shaggy dog stories'
jokes.punchline: Apologizes to actual shaggy dog in bar
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons literal interpretation of idiom - there's actually a shaggy dog present.

jokes.text: And calling us 'dumbasses.' / Which we're so not.
jokes.setup: Complaining about Moe's insults
jokes.punchline: Homer's grammatically awkward denial
jokes.explanation: Mild irony as the protest itself proves Moe's point about their intelligence.

jokes.text: Don't do that.
jokes.setup: Moe attempts to smile
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - implies Moe's smile is so disturbing it's immediately rejected.

jokes.text: I ain't smiled for real since I nailed that rat with the ice pick. / Remember that? That was an amazing throw.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining when he last smiled
jokes.punchline: Violence against vermin as happy memory, Homer's admiration
jokes.explanation: Dark character humor - Moe's violent past as source of joy, Homer's casual acceptance of violence.

jokes.text: What do you call it? Human feeling. / Yeah. Maybe you should... What's the expression? / Go back there. / What's the word I'm searching for? Uh... / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Homer noting Moe shows emotion about school
jokes.punchline: Homer and Barney struggling to find basic words/concepts
jokes.explanation: Good escalating stupidity as both characters struggle with basic concepts like 'emotion' and 'return.'

jokes.text: I'm an urban Lenny!
jokes.setup: Carl volunteering to run bar
jokes.punchline: Defining himself as urban version of Lenny
jokes.explanation: Absurd self-description that plays on Carl and Lenny's interchangeable nature.

jokes.text: Look, I don't want to start a tinkling contest here. / Or do I?
jokes.setup: Moe saying he doesn't want competition
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation leads to actual urination contest consideration
jokes.explanation: Classic malapropism from Moe with good escalation as he considers the literal interpretation.

jokes.text: Oh, don't look so proud. That was wind-assisted.
jokes.setup: Urination contest aftermath
jokes.punchline: Moe diminishing someone's achievement with technical detail
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Moe's petty competitiveness extending to bathroom activities.

jokes.text: No, moe. You've got it all wrong. People buy beer from you.
jokes.setup: Moe saying he has to pay for beer
jokes.punchline: Homer explaining basic business concept to bar owner
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal - Homer having to explain basic business to the business owner.

jokes.text: Hey, what happened to the music? / Don't worry. You got to hit it just right, like fonzie. / Eh! / Oh! Whoa! / Hemorrhage-amundo!
jokes.setup: Jukebox stops working, Homer references Fonzie hitting it
jokes.punchline: Homer's attempt causes injury, creates 'Fonzie-speak' medical term
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy setup with creative portmanteau 'hemorrhage-amundo' combining medical term with Fonzie-speak.

jokes.text: None. A Cosmopolitan is made with cranberry juice.
jokes.setup: Bartending class asking about grenadine in Cosmopolitan
jokes.punchline: Rough bartender Moe knows sophisticated cocktail recipes
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - crude Moe having sophisticated cocktail knowledge.

jokes.text: Moe szyslak, you old glasswipe.
jokes.setup: Professor greeting Moe warmly
jokes.punchline: Affectionate insult using bartending terminology
jokes.explanation: Professional bartending slang used as endearment - 'glasswipe' as term of affection

jokes.text: Yeah. I'd like to speak to a Mr. Tabooger. First name Ollie. / Ooh! Bart! My first prank call. What do I do? / Just ask if anyone knows Ollie tabooger. / I don't get it. / Yell out, 'I'll eat a booger.' / What's the gag? / Oh, forget it!
jokes.setup: Bart making prank call to Homer at bar
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't understand the prank call mechanism
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about prank calls with Homer's obliviousness ruining the classic bit.

jokes.text: When I first saw the movie ironweed, I thought, you know, this is for me.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining his career inspiration
jokes.punchline: Career choice based on movie about alcoholic derelicts
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Moe inspired by depressing movie about alcoholism to become bartender. Dark and character-specific.

jokes.text: Is 'crap-hole' one word? / Yes, if it's hyphenated. / Then I'll stick with crap-hole.
jokes.setup: Professor asks Moe to describe tavern in one word
jokes.punchline: Technical grammar solution to describe his terrible bar
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay solution with good character honesty about his bar's quality.

jokes.text: Do you have a cure for cancer? Because that would be great!
jokes.setup: Dying professor asks if Moe can do anything
jokes.punchline: Moe's hopeful but inappropriate request
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Moe's typical inappropriate optimism in tragic situation.

jokes.text: Hey, don't you want to take your shoes off before you go swimming? / Professor? / Oh.
jokes.setup: Professor walking into pond
jokes.punchline: Moe's practical concern while professor dies
jokes.explanation: Dark humor as Moe misses the obvious suicide attempt, focusing on shoe practicality.

jokes.text: You dirty teen!
jokes.setup: Homer sees someone 'vandalizing' the bar
jokes.punchline: Homer's accusation before realizing it's Moe
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection humor with Homer's instant age-based assumption.

jokes.text: I am formico, the Dean of design. / Hi, formico. / Uh-uh-uh. My name must never be spoken. / Sorry. He seems Nice.
jokes.setup: Designer introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts by forbidding use of his name
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with designer's contradictory introduction rules.

jokes.text: Cecil is a girl's name.
jokes.setup: Meeting bouncer named Cecil
jokes.punchline: Homer's tactless observation
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer tactlessness - predictable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: This place looks like it's from the not-too-distant future. / Yeah. You like it, homer? / Um... the rabbits are cute. / That one ain't moving.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to compliment new bar design
jokes.punchline: One of the 'cute rabbits' is dead
jokes.explanation: Good dark visual gag with Homer's innocent observation leading to morbid discovery.

jokes.text: It's po-mo. / Postmodern. / Yeah, all right. Weird for the sake of weird.
jokes.setup: Explaining artistic concept to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive but accurate summary of postmodernism
jokes.explanation: Excellent satirical observation - Homer's crude but insightful critique of postmodernism. Very quotable.

jokes.text: We don't serve duff no more. We got a Malaysian beer that's better than duff. It's made out of soy sauce.
jokes.setup: Fancy new bar with upscale alternatives
jokes.punchline: Bizarre beer made from condiment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about hipster bar alternatives with disgusting ingredient revelation.

jokes.text: I'm trapped. This is creative design run amok.
jokes.setup: Lenny stuck in artistic bar furniture
jokes.punchline: Articulate critique from usually simple character
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lenny displaying unexpected eloquence in his critique.

jokes.text: After chernobyl, my penis is falling off. / And 'penis' is Russian for...
jokes.setup: Russian model making conversation
jokes.punchline: Disturbing revelation, Moe's attempt to salvage situation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about nuclear disaster effects with Moe's desperate damage control.

jokes.text: Hey! Nobody calls moe St. cool a phony!
jokes.setup: Moe being called a phony
jokes.punchline: Defends fake persona with fake name
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Moe defending against accusation of phoniness using obviously phony name.

jokes.text: You'd be having a great time if you'd stayed in your dark spot.
jokes.setup: Regular customers complaining about feeling like losers
jokes.punchline: Moe basically admits he designed a place to hide ugly customers
jokes.explanation: Moe's complete lack of tact - admits he had designated areas to hide unattractive customers

jokes.text: It's not about spite. It's about petty revenge and getting back at that traitor, moe!
jokes.setup: Homer denying spiteful motivation
jokes.punchline: Describes exactly what spite is with different words
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - denying spite while describing spite in different terms. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Father, give me legs.
jokes.setup: Homer working on bar construction
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic prayer for basic anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Homer's dramatic religious appeal for something he already has.

jokes.text: Father, give me legs.
jokes.setup: Homer running his makeshift bar
jokes.punchline: Dramatic prayer for mobility while presumably lying on floor
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic religious appeal for basic motor function - treats walking as divine gift

jokes.text: Oh, you got one of them cell phones, huh? / Yeah. No cord at all on those.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to relate to hip patron
jokes.punchline: Obvious observation about cordless phones
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's awkward attempts to connect with modern customers.

jokes.text: Unless you're being ironic, turn that off now.
jokes.setup: Sports game being turned on in trendy bar
jokes.punchline: Only acceptable if consumed ironically
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of hipster culture and ironic consumption. Very quotable and accurate.

jokes.text: I won't drink at moe's! Because moe's a big jerk and a she-male, too!
jokes.setup: Homer's protest song about Moe
jokes.punchline: Escalates to inappropriate gender insult
jokes.explanation: Weak humor relying on dated offensive language. Character-consistent but poor craft.

jokes.text: how did you get r. E.M. To play in your garage? / I told them it was a benefit. They think they're saving the rain forest! / Suckers!
jokes.setup: Famous band playing in Homer's garage
jokes.punchline: Homer's deception of environmentally conscious band
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with Homer's casual deception of well-meaning celebrities. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I told them it was a benefit. They think they're saving the rain forest! Suckers!
jokes.setup: How Homer got R.E.M. to play in his garage
jokes.punchline: Homer tricked famous band with fake environmental cause
jokes.explanation: Homer's accidental genius - exploits band's environmental activism for personal bar war

jokes.text: Bar? I see no bar. This is a hunting club.
jokes.setup: Moe questioning Homer's illegal bar
jokes.punchline: Homer's prepared legal loophole
jokes.explanation: Good setup for legal loophole humor, showing Homer's occasional cunning.

jokes.text: That's not the r. E.M. Way.
jokes.setup: Band member getting angry about being tricked
jokes.punchline: Michael Stipe invoking band's peaceful philosophy
jokes.explanation: Self-referential humor about band's reputation for social consciousness and non-violence

jokes.text: That's not the r. E.M. Way. / You're right. / Let's recycle those shards and get out of here.
jokes.setup: REM member about to act violently
jokes.punchline: Band's commitment to environmental responsibility even when angry
jokes.explanation: Good character humor playing on REM's environmental activism extending to all situations.

jokes.text: It's not fair, dad. Why should an animal die just because you and moe are fighting? / It's the law. My hands are tied.
jokes.setup: Lisa's moral objection to hunting
jokes.punchline: Homer treating loophole as unavoidable legal requirement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Homer treating his own scheme as legal obligation.

jokes.text: Lemons.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why animals should die for Homer and Moe's fight
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely inadequate single-word response
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist Homer moment - responds to moral complexity with random fruit. Maximum comedy from minimum words.

jokes.text: Do you really think the Turkey's just gonna climb onto the plate? / I would.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning Homer's thanksgiving setup
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact suicidal ideation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Homer's casual self-deprecation taken to extreme logical conclusion.

jokes.text: Do you really think the Turkey's just gonna climb onto the plate? / I would.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning Homer's dinner preparation logic
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete lack of self-preservation instinct
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - would willingly sacrifice himself for dinner convenience

jokes.text: Turkeys, the only animal smarter than man.
jokes.setup: Homer hunting for turkey
jokes.punchline: Homer's assessment of turkey intelligence
jokes.explanation: Mild irony with Homer inadvertently complimenting turkey intelligence while trying to kill one.

jokes.text: My leg! Oh, geez! / Got that cat right in the leg. / Dad, you shot moe! / Oh, no! This time I really am gonna faint!
jokes.setup: Homer shooting at what he thinks is a cougar
jokes.punchline: Shot his friend, Homer's delayed realization and fainting threat
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation with Homer's pride turning to horror.

jokes.text: It's like my dad always said, 'eventually everybody gets shot.'
jokes.setup: Moe forgiving Homer for shooting him
jokes.punchline: Family wisdom about universal violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark character humor - perfectly captures Moe's fatalistic worldview and rough upbringing. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: All right, I'm sorry. But I Will not save the rain forest!
jokes.setup: Homer apologizing to REM
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on environmental destruction
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Homer's stubborn refusal to do good even when apologizing.

jokes.text: Tofu and gluten. / I'm thankful I ate before I came.
jokes.setup: Describing healthy thanksgiving turkey substitute
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest reaction to terrible food
jokes.explanation: Standard setup-punchline about unappetizing health food, delivered well by Bart.
